channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: SEC just hit four companies with big fines for downplaying the SolarWinds hack
item.description: The Securities and Exchange Commission fined four companies on Tuesday with misleading investors about the impact the 2020 hack of SolarWinds had on their own systems.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Alkira expands NaaS platform with ZTNA capabilities
item.description: Alkira is expanding its namesake network-as-a-service (NaaS) platform with new zero trust network access (ZTNA) capabilities. Alkira provides a SaaS service for on-demand network infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Deception in democracy: Beware the most common types of election-related scams
item.description: Cybersecurity risks are ramping up as the Nov. 5 U.S. general election approaches. Social media and the internet are used to spread propaganda.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As coal plants shut in Romania, some miners transition to green energy while others are reluctant
item.description: PETRILA, Romania (AP) — For many years, Sebastian Tirinticǎ worked in a coal mine, just like his father and grandfather before him.
item.dc:creator: ANCA GURZU, Cipher News, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Meet Capacities, the do-it-all productivity app taking on Notion
item.description: Here’s a tantalizing little paradox to chew over: One of the most interesting up-and-coming productivity apps is also one of the most immediately familiar-feeling—on the surface, at least. The app is called Capacities.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: Crypto's impact on 2024 election will be 'fascinating to see': Mulvaney
item.description: NewsNation political contributor Mick Mulvaney said crypto donations in upcoming elections will be "fascinating.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, The Hill

item.title: The Shift to API-First Thinking: 2024 State of the API Report Reveals Key Trends
item.description: API-first thinking becomes mainstream as developers prioritize API design, integration, and efficiency.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: SSA gets $30M from TMF for IT upgrades key to better customer service
item.description: The Social Security Administration is making IT investments that will make its employees more productive and its services more accessible to its customers. The post SSA gets $30M from TMF for IT upgrades key to better customer service first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: What a Trump win could mean for Elon Musk's businesses
item.description: Elon Musk is wearing multiple hats this election season, sparking questions about how his dual role as a tech leader and vocal surrogate for former President Trump could lead to conflicts of interest.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: New middleware doubles GPU computational efficiency for AI workloads in trials, says Fujitsu
item.description: New middleware from Fujitsu has achieved more than a 2x increase in GPU computational efficiency for artificial intelligence (AI) workloads in trials according to the company, which designed the technology specifically to help solve the issue of GPU limitations and shortages related to computing dem
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Big offshore wind project proposed for New York as other sites are evaluated in 3 states
item.description: BRIGANTINE, N.J. (AP) — Offshore wind energy projects in New York, New Jersey and Maryland are moving forward, as federal regulators examine the proposals and opponents escalate their legal challenges to the work.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How the Army makes software a front and center focus
item.description: "One of the things that we really change as part of the software implementation is, no longer is the Army going to retest everything," said Jennifer Swanson. The post How the Army makes software a front and center focus first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Beware of fake parking tickets on your windshield with fake fines
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how scammers use fake parking ticket texts and receipts to con victims. Learn how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FTC's rule banning fake online reviews goes into effect
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal rule banning fake online reviews is now in effect. The Federal Trade Commission issued the rule in August banning the sale or purchase of online reviews.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State Department making sure change is more than just a name
item.description: Kelly Fletcher, the State Department’s CIO, said the newly named Bureau of Diplomatic Technology is aligning cybersecurity and customer experience. The post State Department making sure change is more than just a name first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Legislation now calling for spacecraft manufacturers to create cybersecurity plan when working with NASA
item.description: The Spacecraft Cybersecurity Act would require NASA to secure a cybersecurity protection. The post Legislation now calling for spacecraft manufacturers to create cybersecurity plan when working with NASA first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: GoFundMe bets social media can unlock Gen Z giving. A Meta partnership and new tools will test that
item.description: New GoFundMe tools will make it easier to circulate causes across online platforms in a push to cater toward younger generations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Cuban says Harris campaign rejected live X interview with Musk
item.description: Billionaire investor Mark Cuban said Monday that Vice President Harris’s campaign rejected a live interview on the social platform X with billionaire Elon Musk, who owns the platform.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Effectiveness of cybersecurity penetration testing depends on what you penetrate
item.description: "We get to do all kinds of really interesting, often nefarious things. Our job is to think like the bad guys do, and to try and break in," said Rob Olson. The post Effectiveness of cybersecurity penetration testing depends on what you penetrate first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Slack researcher discusses the fear, loathing and excitement surrounding AI in the workplace
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Artificial intelligence's recent rise to the forefront of business has left most office workers wondering how often they should use the technology and whether a computer will eventually replace them.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 11 open source AI projects that developers will love
item.description: The most creative and impactful ideas in software often appear as open source. Sharing code invites a global community of contributors to work together to drive a project forward. Artificial intelligence is no exception.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Stanford researchers map ‘White-Only’ properties using AI
item.description: Racially restrictive covenants are clauses in property deeds that ban individuals of a particular race from buying a home.
item.dc:creator: Hamza Fahmy, NewsNation

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it's the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams. “Scams have become so sophisticated now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA MORGA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Chinese humanoid robot could be the future of affordable in-home care
item.description: A humanoid robot made by a Chinese company proposes to redefine personalized elderly care and assistance for people with disabilities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Friday delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition until an appeals court decides whether to block the shake-up because of legal questions surrounding a jury's verdict that branded Google as an illegal monopolist.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: DoD ‘fine tunes’ final CMMC program rule, industry turns attention to implementation
item.description: The Pentagon made some key tweaks to the final CMMC program rule. Several program watchers said the changes should help simplify a complex compliance program. The post DoD ‘fine tunes’ final CMMC program rule, industry turns attention to implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Alabama man arrested in SEC social media account hack that led the price of bitcoin to spike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Alabama man was arrested Thursday for his alleged role in the January hack of a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission social media account that led the price of bitcoin to spike, the Justice Department said. Eric Council Jr.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hit the lights: Mets outfit Citi Field for electric light shows
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Mets are lighting up Citi Field, even before their batters step up to the plate. About 70 front-office staffers scattered around the ballpark for over five hours to install 3,500 strips of LED lights on each railing ahead of New York's first home postseason game.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US agency adopts rule to make it easier for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission adopted a final rule Wednesday that will require businesses to make it easy for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions and memberships.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
